mission.title: Install and Configure Openfga on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenFGA is a high-performance and flexible authorization/permission engine built for developers, inspired by Google Zanzibar. Installing OpenFGA allows you to manage and enforce fine-grained access control in your applications.

steps.title: Add the OpenFGA Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenFGA Helm chart repository to your Helm client to access the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo add openfga https://openfga.dev/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenFGA using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenFGA in a new namespace called 'openfga'. This command will deploy OpenFGA with the specified version.
```bash
helm install openfga openfga/openfga --version 1.11.6 --namespace openfga --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenFGA pods are running successfully in the 'openfga' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfga
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the OpenFGA deployment to set resource limits for better performance and stability.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment openfga --namespace openfga --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for local access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the OpenFGA service to access it from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/openfga --namespace openfga 8080:8080
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OpenFGA will show the OpenFGA pods running in the 'openfga' namespace. You can access the service locally via port 8080.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add openfga https://openfga.dev/charts
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment openfga --namespace openfga --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenFGA release from the cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources, but not CRDs or persistent resources yet.
```bash
helm uninstall openfga --namespace openfga
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation of OpenFGA. This ensures that all resources are cleaned up.
```bash
kubectl delete crd openfgas.openfga.dev
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace openfga
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'openfga' namespace to remove any remaining resources and verify that it has been deleted successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace openfga
kubectl get namespaces | grep openfga
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and any important data. You can use the following command to export the current Helm release configuration.
```bash
helm get values openfga --namespace openfga > openfga-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts and then upgrade OpenFGA to the desired version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade openfga openfga/openfga --version 1.12.0 --namespace openfga
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, check if the OpenFGA pods are running the new version successfully in the 'openfga' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfga
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see that the OpenFGA pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more details on the failure.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace openfga
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the OpenFGA service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and that the service is running.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace openfga
kubectl port-forward service/openfga --namespace openfga 8080:8080
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenFGA pods are not starting due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits set on the deployment. You may need to adjust them based on your cluster capacity.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment openfga --namespace openfga
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails, check for any errors in the output. You can also check the current Helm release status and rollback if necessary.
```bash
helm status openfga --namespace openfga
helm rollback openfga <revision-number> --namespace openfga
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
